before_code:
def E₄E₆Weight : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![4, 6]
-- used as `E₄E₆Weight` in 13 places, no _apply/_def lemmas
after_code: -- inline at every site as `(![4, 6] : Fin 2 → ℕ)`
description: A `def foo : T := value` with no surrounding API (no `_apply`/`_def` lemmas, no users outside one local proof) is junk. Inline `value` at use sites — even if a type ascription `(value : T)` is required for consistent elaboration across rewrites.
Never add subsection dividers like `/-! ### Foo -/` inside a Lean file. Every section header beyond the top-level module docstring is rejected. `/cleanup` must strip them.

before_code:
-- now apply the IH
  exact ih ...
  -- use linearity
  rw [...]
description: Narrative inline comments inside proofs (`-- now apply the IH`, `-- use linearity`, etc.) are rejected. Only `--` comments documenting non-obvious WHY/workarounds survive. `/cleanup` must strip them.

before_code:
/-- For even `k ≥ 4`, there exist `a, b ∈ ℕ` with `4a + 6b = k`. -/
private lemma exists_monomial_weight ...
description: Docstrings (`/-- ... -/`) are reserved for key public declarations only. Private/auxiliary lemmas should have no docstring. `/cleanup` must strip them.

after_code:
subst hk
  rfl
  rw [a, b]  -- combine sequential rw into one
description: One tactic per line. Never `tac1; tac2` on one line. Combine sequential `rw [a]; rw [b]` into `rw [a, b]` (still one tactic), but distinct tactics like `rw [...]; ring` go on separate lines.

Mathlib spells things out. Forbid abbreviations in declaration names: `whomog` → `weightedHomogeneous`, `mvpoly` → `mvPolynomial`, `wt` → `weight`, `soln` → `solution`, `imp` → fully-spelled conclusion (`imp_eval_eq_zero` etc.).

after_code:
-- at the call site:
obtain ⟨d₀, hd0a, hd0b⟩ : ∃ d : Fin 2 →₀ ℕ, d 0 = a ∧ d 1 = b :=
  ⟨Finsupp.equivFunOnFinite.invFun ![a, b], rfl, rfl⟩
description: Trivial single-use existence lemmas like `∃ d, d 0 = a ∧ d 1 = b := ⟨_, rfl, rfl⟩` are junk if used once — write the witness inline at the call site.

before_code:
private lemma mul_modularForm_ne_zero_of_qExpansion_coeff_zero_eq_one
    {k₁ k₂ : ℤ} (f : ModularForm 𝒮ℒ k₁) (g : ModularForm 𝒮ℒ k₂)
    (hf : (qExpansion 1 f).coeff 0 = 1) (hg : (qExpansion 1 g).coeff 0 = 1) :
    f.mul g ≠ 0
after_code:
private lemma mul_ne_zero {k₁ k₂ : ℤ}
    (f : ModularForm 𝒮ℒ k₁) (g : ModularForm 𝒮ℒ k₂) (hf : f ≠ 0) (hg : g ≠ 0) :
    f.mul g ≠ 0 := by
  rw [Ne, ← qExpansion_eq_zero_iff ..., qExpansion_mul ..., mul_eq_zero, not_or]
  ...
description: When proving `f * g ≠ 0`, don't introduce custom q-expansion hypotheses. Use the integral-domain structure: `f ≠ 0`, `g ≠ 0` is enough, deriving via `qExpansion_eq_zero_iff` + the fact that `PowerSeries ℂ` is an integral domain.

after_code:
-- extract each substantive `have` block as a separate private lemma
-- with a descriptive name, then call it
description: Proofs longer than ~30 lines should be broken down. Extract sub-`have` blocks as separate private lemmas with descriptive names. Long monolithic proofs are rejected.

before_code: private lemma cast_modularForm_apply {k₁ k₂ : ℤ} (heq : k₁ = k₂) (h : ModularForm 𝒮ℒ k₁) (z : ℍ) :
after_code:
private lemma cast_modularForm_apply {k₁ k₂ : ℤ} (heq : k₁ = k₂) (h : ModularForm 𝒮ℒ k₁)
    (z : ℍ) :
description: Lines must be ≤100 chars. Break declaration signatures across multiple lines (parameters and result type each on their own continuation) so every line stays under the limit.

before_code:
show g z / d z * d z = g z
  rw [div_mul_cancel₀ _ ...]
after_code:
change g z / d z * d z = g z
  rw [div_mul_cancel₀ _ ...]
description: In tactic mode, when the tactic actually changes the goal (rather than just annotating it for the reader), use `change`, not `show`. The `linter.style.show` linter enforces this.
`push_neg` is deprecated in favour of `push Not at h` (or `push Not` with a location). Replace `push_neg` everywhere.
